questions.original_question: 路特斯科技和路特斯汽车的关系是什么？
questions.answer: 路特斯汽车是跑车生产主体，而路特斯科技是路特斯品牌、IP、全球营销、以及资本市场运作主体，也是纳斯达克上市主体（LOT US）。路特斯汽车的母公司英国路特斯与路特斯科技同属路特斯集团。以上信息基于2025年1月15日之前的知识。

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology is a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports car vehicles for daily usage) under the iconic British brand “Lotus” and sells all Lotus-branded cars. With over seven decades of racing heritage and proven leadership in the automotive industry, the Lotus brand symbolizes market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. Fusing proprietary next-generation technology built on world-class research and development capabilities and an asset-light model empowered by Geely Holding, Lotus Technology is breaking new ground in electrification, digitization, and intelligence. The Lotus brand was founded in the U.K. in 1948 and has since established itself as a high-performance sports car brand with innovative engineering and cutting-edge technologies, renowned for its superior aerodynamics and lightweight design. The Lotus brand was born out of legendary success on the racetrack, including 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles and many other championship honors. In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK and further positioned the company as a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) maker.

rag_info.chunk_content: Positioned as the only Geely Holding-affiliated brand with sports car DNA, Lotus Technology has received comprehensive support from Geely Holding in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), logistics infrastructure, and human capital, and is in the process of transforming from a British sports car company to a global pioneer of high-performance electric vehicles to bridge the gap between the traditional sports car and a new generation of electric vehicles. Lotus Technology expects the business combination with LCAA, a special purpose acquisition company (SPAC) affiliated with L Catterton, which has a strategic relationship with LVMH, to provide significant support in consumer insights and brand collaboration that will enable Lotus Technology to effectively raise its brand awareness globally. This relationship provides L Catterton with direct access to LVMH’s most senior executives, global consumer insights, industry network and contacts, differentiated investment deal flow, and collaboration on operating best practices. L Catterton retains sole discretion over all investment and portfolio management decisions related to the L Catterton funds. As of the date of this prospectus, Lotus Technology has not entered into any agreements with LVMH.

rag_info.chunk_content:
[Table Level]  
- **Table Title**: Lotus Technology Corporate Structure Overview  
- **Table Summary**: This diagram provides a visual representation of the corporate structure of Lotus Technology Inc. as of the date of the annual report. It outlines the relationships and equity ownership between Lotus Technology Inc., its offshore subsidiaries, and its onshore subsidiaries in various regions including Hong Kong, the UK, Germany, the Netherlands, and mainland China.  
- **Context**: Before the diagram, the text discusses caution regarding forward-looking statements and mentions the dynamic risks inherent in Lotus Technology Inc.’s operations. After the diagram, it describes Lotus Technology Inc.’s Cayman Islands holding framework and its operational transitions, particularly changes following the 2023 Restructuring.  
- **Special Notes**: The diagram makes a clear distinction between “Offshore” and “Onshore” entities using a dashed line. All entities are shown with 100% equity interest relationships.  

[Row Level]  
- **Row 1**: Lotus Technology Inc., incorporated in the Cayman Islands, is the parent holding company at the top of the corporate structure.  
- **Row 2**: Lotus Technology International Limited (Hong Kong) and Lotus Advanced Technology Limited (Hong Kong) are wholly owned subsidiaries of Lotus Technology Inc. These subsidiaries represent “Offshore” entities in the corporate hierarchy.  
- **Row 3**: Lotus Advanced Technology Limited holds 100% equity interest in Wuhan Lotus Technology Co., Ltd., which is delineated as an “Onshore” subsidiary in mainland China.  
- **Row 4**: Wuhan Lotus Technology Co., Ltd. has multiple wholly owned onshore subsidiaries: Wuhan Lotus Cars Co., Ltd., Wuhan Lotus Cars Sales Limited, Wuhan Lotus Private Fund Management Co., Ltd., Sanya Lotus Venture Capital Co., Ltd., and Hangzhou Lotus Technology Service Co., Ltd.  
- **Row 5**: In addition to its onshore connections, Lotus Advanced Technology Limited also holds 100% ownership in several offshore subsidiaries: Lotus Tech Creative Centre Limited (UK), Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH (Germany), and Lotus Cars Europe B.V. (Netherlands).  
- **Row 6**: Lotus Technology Innovative Limited (UK) is another offshore subsidiary directly owned by Lotus Technology Inc.  

This corporate structure shows clear ownership chains and demarcates offshore and onshore entities within Lotus Technology Inc.’s operational framework.

rag_info.chunk_content: 1987 Esprit Type 85. Very few people seem to realize that this version of the Esprit was still being produced under the Type 85 designation, most assuming that all Esprit models were now coming from the factory with turbocharged engines and coded as the Type 82. In fact, the difference between the Esprit Turbo and the normally aspirated (n/a) base model was hard to spot for almost anybody except the real hard-core Lotus enthusiast. The new style car, code-named X180 by the factory, had been the work of a team of designers under the control of Lotus design director Colin Spooner and led by Peter Stevens, ably supported by Ken Sears. The fruits of their work were first seen when the new shape car was launched at the British Motor Show in October 1987, with delivery of the first cars going out to customers almost immediately. With the car being primarily a new bodyshell on top of an existing and well-proven chassis/engine combination, its development from start to finish had taken just 15 months, an incredibly short time to design what to the outside world seemed to be a new car.

Lotus Technology expects its relationship with Geely Holding to allow the company to bring its vehicles to the market at an accelerated pace by leveraging Geely Holding’s manufacturing capacity, bargaining power in procurement and supply chain, capital investment, and operational support. Lotus Technology also provides Geely Holding with the intellectual properties necessary for vehicle production. Geely Holding is mainly responsible for the ordering and inspection of raw materials, production planning, production quality control, logistics and transportation of manufactured vehicles, and construction and operation of the manufacturing plant.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Manufacturing, Supply Chain, and Quality Control 
summary: Our collaboration with Geely Holding is a strategic partnership that plays a crucial role in our vehicle development process. By leveraging Geely's manufacturing capabilities and market presence alongside our technological advancements, we aim to drive innovation and accelerate the transition to sustainable mobility solutions, including electric vehicles. This partnership enhances our asset-light business model and facilitates the market introduction of our vehicles through Geely's procurement advantages and operational support. 

Under a 10-year manufacturing agreement initiated in 2022, Geely Holding has been authorized to access our technologies while we retain oversight of design, development, and brand standards. Geely is responsible for sourcing materials, production planning, quality control, and logistics, all while adhering to our quality assurance framework. This collaboration emphasizes research and development initiatives to create cutting-edge products that meet consumer demands and promote environmental sustainability, ultimately strengthening our competitive edge in the global automotive market.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Manufacturing, Supply Chain, and Quality Control 
summary: The "Manufacturing, Supply Chain, and Quality Control" chapter highlights the critical role of manufacturers and suppliers in the vehicle development process, emphasizing the necessity of strict adherence to quality standards. A strategic partnership with Geely Holding, the major shareholder, underpins an asset-light business model that accelerates market entry by leveraging Geely's extensive manufacturing capacity, procurement power, capital investment, and operational support.

In 2022, a significant milestone was reached with the establishment of a 10-year manufacturing agreement with Geely Holding for vehicle production. This agreement delineates responsibilities, with the company retaining control over design, development, supplier designation, product announcements, and the maintenance of Lotus brand standards. Conversely, Geely Holding is responsible for sourcing raw materials, production planning, quality control, logistics, and plant operations, ensuring that quality assurance aligns with the company's established framework.

The manufacturing facility located in Wuhan, China, owned by Geely Holding, is specifically tailored for electric vehicles (EVs) and spans over one million square meters. This state-of-the-art plant is equipped with advanced manufacturing technologies, including stamping, welding, painting, assembly, and a dedicated quality inspection center. Additionally, the facility integrates intelligent driving technology for vehicle transport within workshops and features a 3 km track for quality testing, enabling high-speed evaluations of up to 230 km/h and providing various driving experiences for customers.

Overall, this chapter underscores the strategic collaboration with Geely Holding as a cornerstone of the manufacturing and supply chain strategy, ensuring that the company can efficiently produce high-quality vehicles while maintaining brand integrity and meeting market demands.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Manufacturing, Supply Chain, and Quality Control 
summary: The company recognizes its manufacturers and suppliers as vital partners in the vehicle development process, aiming to uphold high-quality standards through their industry expertise. Our strategic partnership with Geely Holding enhances this approach by combining our strengths in automotive innovation and technology. This collaboration focuses on developing advanced electric vehicle (EV) solutions, promoting sustainable mobility, and expanding our market presence. By leveraging Geely's manufacturing capabilities and operational support, we anticipate a faster market entry for our vehicles.

Geely will manage production logistics, quality control, and certification processes, all aligned with our quality assurance framework. Together, we are committed to driving the future of mobility, enhancing our competitive edge, and contributing to a greener transportation landscape.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: We are a leading global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, specializing in the design and development of luxury lifestyle vehicles under the esteemed British brand "Lotus." With a rich racing heritage spanning over 70 years, Lotus is renowned for its high-performance standards in both design and engineering. Since Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK in 2017, we have successfully transitioned into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer, benefiting from Geely's support across various operational areas.

Our flagship model, the Eletre, is a luxury E-segment SUV that showcases our innovative 800-volt Electrical Performance Architecture (EPA). The Eletre is available in three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R stands out with an impressive 905 horsepower, achieving acceleration from 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds, and offers a WLTP range of 490 km, complemented by rapid charging capabilities. Deliveries of the Eletre commenced in 2023, and we launched our second model, the Emeya, in September 2023, with deliveries set to begin in March 2024. Furthermore, we have plans to introduce two additional models in the near future.

Our research and development capabilities, deeply rooted in Lotus's sports car legacy, serve as a significant competitive advantage, exemplified by the five-year development of the EPA, which facilitates rapid model rollout and enhances production efficiency. Additionally, we have developed an advanced Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS) featuring Level 4-ready hardware and a deployable LiDAR system.

To enhance customer experience, we employ a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating premium stores in high-traffic areas alongside a global digital platform for vehicle customization and purchasing. As of June 30, 2024, our global distribution network comprises 207 stores. In 2023, we delivered a total of 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024. Our workforce consists of 2,989 full-time employees, with a substantial 54.6% dedicated to research and development. 

This comprehensive approach positions us at the forefront of the luxury electric vehicle market, driving innovation and excellence in mobility solutions.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Business 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Our Business**

Lotus is a premier global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, renowned for designing and developing luxury lifestyle vehicles under its iconic British brand. Established in the U.K. in 1948, Lotus has a rich heritage of over seven decades in racing, symbolizing excellence in performance, design, and engineering, with a legacy that includes 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles.

In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK, transforming the company into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer. This strategic partnership enhances Lotus's capabilities in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), and logistics, facilitating a transition from a traditional sports car company to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles. The luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only about 10 existing models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine luxury models, positioning Lotus to capitalize on first-mover advantages.

Lotus launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022, with deliveries commencing in 2023. The Eletre features an advanced 800-volt EPA platform, offering three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R achieves an impressive 905 horsepower and accelerates from 0-100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery provides a WLTP range of 490 km and can be charged from 10% to 80% in under 20 minutes, with an average MSRP exceeding $100,000. Following the Eletre, the second model, the Emeya, was launched in September 2023, with deliveries expected to begin in March 2024.

Lotus's R&D capabilities are a competitive strength, highlighted by the proprietary 800-volt EPA developed over five years, which supports rapid model rollout and production scalability. The company also features advanced ADAS technology, including the first deployable LiDAR system. R&D facilities in Europe and China foster collaboration among experienced teams, enhancing innovation.

All BEV lifestyle models are manufactured through a contract partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art EV manufacturing facilities in Wuhan, China. This partnership enhances scalability and flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments. Access to Geely’s global supply chain allows Lotus to secure high-quality components at competitive prices, effectively managing supply chain risks.

Lotus's sales strategy emphasizes a digital-first, omni-channel approach, providing a luxury retail experience through premium stores and a comprehensive digital platform. As of June 30, 2024, the company has established a global distribution network with 207 stores and a Global Commercial Platform for distributing Lotus vehicles, including the Eletre and Emeya. In 2023, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024, encompassing both lifestyle and sports cars.

This comprehensive approach positions Lotus as a leader in the luxury BEV market, leveraging its rich heritage, innovative technology, and strategic partnerships to drive future growth.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of the Overview Chapter**

We are a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer operating under the iconic British brand "Lotus," which boasts over 70 years of racing heritage. Since our acquisition by Geely Holding in 2017, we have been transitioning from a traditional sports car company to a luxury lifestyle BEV maker, leveraging Geely's extensive support in manufacturing, supply chain, and research and development (R&D).

The global luxury BEV market, defined as vehicles with a Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) exceeding $80,000, is projected to experience significant growth, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 35% from 2021 to 2031, ultimately reaching nearly 1.9 million units by 2031. Currently, this market is underserved, featuring only about 10 luxury BEV models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models.

Our first fully electric model, the Eletre, launched in 2022, is a luxury E-segment SUV powered by our proprietary 800-volt Electric Performance Architecture (EPA). The Eletre is available in three versions, with the Eletre R delivering an impressive 905 horsepower and achieving 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. We aim to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027, having launched our second lifestyle vehicle, the Emeya, in September 2023, with deliveries scheduled to commence in March 2024. Future product launches include a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV lifestyle sports car in 2026.

Our R&D capabilities serve as a competitive strength, supported by five facilities located in the U.K., Germany, and China. These facilities focus on developing advanced technologies, including a leading Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS). We manufacture our BEV models in partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art facilities in Wuhan, China, with a planned annual production capacity of 150,000 units.

We employ a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating 215 stores globally as of December 31, 2023, with plans to expand to over 300 by 2025. In 2023, we successfully delivered 6,970 vehicles, encompassing both lifestyle and sports car models. Our collaboration with L Catterton, which has connections to LVMH, is anticipated to enhance our brand awareness and consumer insights, although no formal agreements with LVMH have been finalized as of this prospectus.

This strategic positioning within the luxury BEV market, combined with our innovative product offerings and robust R&D capabilities, positions us well for future growth and success in the evolving automotive landscape.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: Lotus is an innovative luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer, renowned for creating high-end lifestyle vehicles that blend performance, design, and engineering excellence, backed by over 70 years of racing heritage. Founded in the U.K. in 1948, Lotus has transitioned from a traditional sports car manufacturer to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles, especially after Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in 2017. This partnership has facilitated significant advancements in manufacturing, research and development (R&D), and logistics, enabling Lotus to focus on electrification and intelligent automotive solutions.

The global luxury BEV market, projected to grow at a CAGR of 35% from 2021 to 2031, is currently underserved, with only about 10 luxury BEV models available compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) models. Lotus aims to capitalize on this gap by launching its E-segment BEV model, the Hyper-SUV Eletre, which debuted in 2022. The Eletre features an innovative 800-volt Electric Powertrain Architecture, offering exceptional performance, rapid charging capabilities, and a competitive price point. The company plans to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027, with additional models like the Emeya and a D-segment SUV set for future release.

Lotus emphasizes its R&D capabilities as a competitive advantage, leveraging its proprietary technologies and advanced driver assistance systems. The company operates five R&D facilities across the U.K., Germany, and China, fostering collaboration among skilled teams. Manufacturing is conducted in partnership with Geely at advanced facilities in Wuhan, China, which allows for scalability and flexibility while minimizing capital investments.

To enhance customer experience, Lotus employs a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, featuring premium stores and a global digital platform that includes a virtual showroom and customization options. The brand has established a global distribution network, with plans to expand its store count from 215 to over 300 by 2025. In 2023, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles, reflecting its commitment to leading the electrification of luxury vehicles while maintaining strong ties to its British heritage.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology is a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) maker that designs, develops, and sells luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports car vehicles for daily usage) under the iconic British brand “Lotus.” With over seven decades of racing heritage and proven leadership in the automotive industry, the Lotus brand symbolizes the market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. Fusing proprietary next-generation technology built on world-class research and development capabilities and an asset-light model empowered by Geely Holding, Lotus Technology is breaking new ground in electrification, digitization, and intelligence. The mailing address of Lotus Technology’s principal executive office is No. 800 Century Avenue, Pudong District, Shanghai, People’s Republic of China, and the phone number is +86 21 5466-6258. Lotus Technology’s corporate website address is www.group-lotus.com. Lotus Technology’s website and the information contained on, or that can be accessed through, the website are not deemed to be incorporated by reference in, and are not considered part of, this proxy statement/prospectus.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Tech 
summary: Lotus Tech is a cutting-edge luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer known for its high-end lifestyle vehicles under the prestigious British brand "Lotus." With a legacy of over 70 years in racing and a strong automotive reputation, Lotus embodies excellence in performance, design, and engineering. The company leverages advanced proprietary technology and robust research and development, operating under an asset-light model supported by Geely Holding. Lotus Tech is leading the way in electrification, digitization, and intelligent vehicle solutions. Its principal office is located in Shanghai, China, and further information can be found on their corporate website, though it is not part of the official proxy statement/prospectus.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The Lotus brand is closely associated with “customer engagement” and “community building,” and in order to provide customers with a luxurious experience, Lotus Technology has adopted a direct-to-consumer global sales and distribution strategy that focuses on establishing and developing direct relationships with customers, especially in selected regions including China, Europe, and the U.S., which represent some of the key regions that drive fast growth in the global battery electric vehicle markets. By offering a luxurious purchasing experience and superior customer service, Lotus Technology expects to further deepen its penetration in the global market and targets to establish a total of over 300 stores globally by 2025. Develop next-generation electric vehicle technologies while monetizing Lotus Technology's R&D prowess. Lotus Technology intends to continuously develop and enhance proprietary cutting-edge technologies, including Lotus Technology's 800-volt EPA architecture and hardware, algorithm, and software systems to enhance the competitiveness of Lotus Technology's vehicles, supported by continuous R&D investment. All of Lotus Technology's proprietary technologies are built with the principle of “For the Drivers,” while inheriting Lotus Technology's unique design language and philosophy. Lotus Technology is in the process of transforming from a British sports car company to a global pioneer of high-performance lifestyle electric vehicles and has set high quality and safety standards for and makes continuous improvements on Lotus Technology's vehicles and technologies, including Lotus Technology's sensory hardware and ADAS software.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Strategy 
summary: The global luxury car market is set to grow significantly, with a projected compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 10% from 2021 to 2031, while the battery electric vehicle (BEV) sector is expected to expand even more rapidly at a CAGR of 24%. Within this context, the luxury BEV segment is anticipated to surge at a remarkable CAGR of 35%, aiming for a market size of 1.9 million units by 2025. 

To capitalize on this growth, Lotus is implementing a strategic transformation focused on enhancing its brand equity and expanding its electric vehicle portfolio. By leveraging its rich racing heritage, Lotus aims to position itself as a leader in the high-performance electric automotive market, targeting affluent consumers and tech-savvy younger generations. The company plans to deepen its market presence through a direct-to-consumer sales strategy, establishing over 300 stores globally by 2025, particularly in key regions like China, Europe, and the U.S.

Lotus is committed to advancing next-generation electric vehicle technologies, emphasizing high quality and safety, while continuously improving its proprietary systems such as the 800-volt EPA architecture and advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS). The company also aims to monetize its R&D capabilities by licensing technologies and offering ADAS software subscriptions.

In line with its sustainability goals, Lotus has launched its E-segment BEV model ahead of competitors and plans to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027, with a target of carbon neutrality by 2038. The company is dedicated to leading the electrification of the luxury car segment through innovative vehicle launches, including a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV lifestyle sports car in 2026, while focusing on waste reduction and the use of renewable resources in its operations.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology Inc. has operations across the UK, the EU, and China. Lotus Technology Inc. is dedicated to delivering luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicles, with a focus on world-class research and development in next-generation automobility technologies such as electrification, digitalisation, and more. For more information about Lotus Technology Inc., please visit www.group-lotus.com.

title: About Lotus Technology Inc. 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. is a luxury electric vehicle manufacturer with operations in the UK, EU, and China. all_retrieved_content.page_content: Positioned as the only Geely Holding-affiliated brand with sports car DNA, Lotus Technology has received comprehensive support from Geely Holding in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), logistics infrastructure, and human capital, and is in the process of transforming from a British sports car company to a global pioneer of high-performance electric vehicles to bridge the gap between the traditional sports car and a new generation of electric vehicles. The proposed business combination with LCAA, a special purpose acquisition company (SPAC) affiliated with L Catterton, which has a strategic relationship with LVMH, is expected to provide significant support in consumer insights and brand collaboration that will enable Lotus Technology to effectively raise its brand awareness globally. L Catterton has an exclusive relationship for consumer-focused private equity with LVMH and Financière Agache, the main investment company of Bernard Arnault, LVMH’s chairman and chief executive officer, and his family. This relationship provides L Catterton with direct access to LVMH’s most senior executives, global consumer insights, industry network and contacts, differentiated investment deal flow, and collaboration on operating best practices. L Catterton retains sole discretion over all investment and portfolio management decisions related to the L Catterton funds. As of the date of this proxy statement/prospectus, Lotus Technology has not entered into any agreements with LVMH.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our business 
summary: The company is a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer operating under the prestigious British brand "Lotus," which has a rich history of over 70 years in racing and automotive excellence. Acquired by Geely Holding in 2017, Lotus is transitioning from a traditional sports car manufacturer to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles, supported by Geely's asset-light model and extensive R&D capabilities. The luxury BEV market is projected to grow significantly, and Lotus aims to capitalize on this opportunity by launching its E-segment BEV model, the Hyper-SUV Eletre, which debuted in 2022. The Eletre features advanced technology, impressive performance metrics, and a competitive price point, contributing to a strong global order book of over 8,600 units as of September 30, 2023.

In addition to the Eletre, the company plans to introduce the Emeya lifestyle vehicle and two more fully electric models by 2025. Its proprietary 800-volt Electric Powertrain Architecture enhances vehicle performance and production efficiency, while a cutting-edge Driver Assistance System incorporates advanced safety features. Manufacturing is conducted in partnership with Geely in China, leveraging their facilities and supply chain to ensure scalability and quality.

The company employs a digital-first, omni-channel sales strategy, enhancing customer engagement through personalized services and a global distribution network. With 206 stores worldwide and plans to expand to over 300 by 2025, Lotus is well-positioned to enhance its brand presence and meet the growing demand for luxury BEVs, supported by a cumulative order book exceeding 19,000 units as of late September 2023.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of the Overview Chapter**

We are a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer operating under the iconic British brand "Lotus," which boasts over 70 years of racing heritage. Since our acquisition by Geely Holding in 2017, we have been transitioning from a traditional sports car company to a luxury lifestyle BEV maker, leveraging Geely's extensive support in manufacturing, supply chain, and research and development (R&D).

The global luxury BEV market, defined as vehicles with a Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) exceeding $80,000, is projected to experience significant growth, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 35% from 2021 to 2031, ultimately reaching nearly 1.9 million units by 2031. Currently, this market is underserved, featuring only about 10 luxury BEV models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models.

Our first fully electric model, the Eletre, launched in 2022, is a luxury E-segment SUV powered by our proprietary 800-volt Electric Performance Architecture (EPA). The Eletre is available in three versions, with the Eletre R delivering an impressive 905 horsepower and achieving 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. We aim to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027, having launched our second lifestyle vehicle, the Emeya, in September 2023, with deliveries scheduled to commence in March 2024. Future product launches include a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV lifestyle sports car in 2026.

Our R&D capabilities serve as a competitive strength, supported by five facilities located in the U.K., Germany, and China. These facilities focus on developing advanced technologies, including a leading Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS). We manufacture our BEV models in partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art facilities in Wuhan, China, with a planned annual production capacity of 150,000 units.

We employ a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating 215 stores globally as of December 31, 2023, with plans to expand to over 300 by 2025. In 2023, we successfully delivered 6,970 vehicles, encompassing both lifestyle and sports car models. Our collaboration with L Catterton, which has connections to LVMH, is anticipated to enhance our brand awareness and consumer insights, although no formal agreements with LVMH have been finalized as of this prospectus.

This strategic positioning within the luxury BEV market, combined with our innovative product offerings and robust R&D capabilities, positions us well for future growth and success in the evolving automotive landscape.

Lotus Technology expects the business combination with LCAA, a SPAC affiliated with L Catterton, which has a strategic relationship with LVMH, to provide significant support in consumer insights and brand collaboration that will enable the company to effectively raise brand awareness globally. L Catterton has an exclusive relationship for consumer-focused private equity with LVMH and Financière Agache, the main investment company of Bernard Arnault, LVMH’s chairman and chief executive officer, and his family. This relationship provides L Catterton with direct access to LVMH’s most senior executives, global consumer insights, industry network and contacts, differentiated investment deal flow, and collaboration on operating best practices. L Catterton retains sole discretion over all investment and portfolio management decisions related to the L Catterton funds. As of the date of this prospectus, Lotus Technology has not entered into any agreements with LVMH. According to Oliver Wyman, the global luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, as defined by BEVs with MSRP of over US$80,000, is expected to grow rapidly at a CAGR of 35% over 2021-2031 and reach a market size of nearly 1.9 million units by 2031.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Business 
summary: We are a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer under the esteemed British brand "Lotus," which has over 70 years of racing heritage and is renowned for its performance, design, and engineering excellence. With the support of Geely Holding, which acquired a 51% stake in Lotus in 2017, we are transitioning from traditional sports cars to high-performance electric vehicles, leveraging cutting-edge technology and a robust research and development framework. 

The global luxury BEV market, projected to grow significantly, is currently underserved, with only a handful of luxury BEV models available compared to a multitude of internal combustion engine options. As a leader in this sector, we aim to launch our E-segment BEV model and achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027. Our first fully electric model, the Hyper-SUV Eletre, debuted in 2022, showcasing advanced technology and exceptional performance, with plans for further electric vehicle introductions in the coming years.

Our proprietary 800-volt Electric Power Architecture enables rapid model introductions and efficient production, while our Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS) incorporates cutting-edge features like deployable LiDAR. Manufacturing is conducted in partnership with Geely at their advanced facilities in Wuhan, China, enhancing scalability and supply chain management.

We prioritize a luxury retail experience through a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating premium stores and a global digital platform that enables vehicle customization and direct customer engagement. With a growing global distribution network and plans to expand our store presence, we delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023, including both lifestyle and sports cars, and are poised for continued growth in the luxury BEV market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The board of directors will determine whether, to what extent, at what times and places, and under what conditions or regulations the accounts and books of Lotus Technology will be open to inspection by shareholders. No shareholder will otherwise have any right to inspect any account, book, or document of Lotus Technology except as required by law or authorized by the board of directors or the shareholders by special resolution. Lotus Technology may from time to time by ordinary resolution: ● increase the share capital by such sum, to be divided into shares of such classes and amount, as the resolution will prescribe; ● consolidate and divide all or any share capital into shares of a larger amount than existing shares; ● sub-divide the existing shares or any of them into shares of a smaller amount; provided that in the subdivision the proportion between the amount paid and the amount, if any, unpaid on each reduced share will be the same as it was in the case of the share from which the reduced share is derived;

metadata.title_summary:
title: Inspection of Books and Records 
summary: The chapter on "Inspection of Books and Records" outlines the protocols established by the board of directors regarding shareholder access to the company's financial documents. The board holds the authority to define the conditions under which shareholders may inspect the company's accounts and books, including the extent of access, timing, location, and specific regulations governing such inspections. 

It is important to note that shareholders do not possess an inherent right to inspect company documents. Access is contingent upon either legal mandates, explicit authorization from the board, or approval through a special resolution passed by the shareholders. This framework ensures that the inspection process is regulated and aligned with both corporate governance and legal requirements.

In summary, the board of directors plays a crucial role in determining the parameters for document inspection, thereby safeguarding the company's interests while also adhering to applicable laws that may grant shareholders certain rights.

all_retrieved_content.page_content:
[Table Level]  
- **Table Title**: Lotus Technology Corporate Structure Overview  
- **Table Summary**: This diagram provides a visual representation of the corporate structure of Lotus Technology Inc. as of the date of the annual report. It outlines the relationships and equity ownership between Lotus Technology Inc., its offshore subsidiaries, and its onshore subsidiaries in various regions including Hong Kong, the UK, Germany, the Netherlands, and mainland China.  
- **Context**: Before the diagram, the text discusses caution regarding forward-looking statements and mentions the dynamic risks inherent in Lotus Technology Inc.’s operations. After the diagram, it describes Lotus Technology Inc.’s Cayman Islands holding framework and its operational transitions, particularly changes following the 2023 Restructuring.  
- **Special Notes**: The diagram makes a clear distinction between “Offshore” and “Onshore” entities using a dashed line. All entities are shown with 100% equity interest relationships.  

[Row Level]  
- **Row 1**: Lotus Technology Inc., incorporated in the Cayman Islands, is the parent holding company at the top of the corporate structure.  
- **Row 2**: Lotus Technology International Limited (Hong Kong) and Lotus Advanced Technology Limited (Hong Kong) are wholly owned subsidiaries of Lotus Technology Inc. These subsidiaries represent “Offshore” entities in the corporate hierarchy.  
- **Row 3**: Lotus Advanced Technology Limited holds 100% equity interest in Wuhan Lotus Technology Co., Ltd., which is delineated as an “Onshore” subsidiary in mainland China.  
- **Row 4**: Wuhan Lotus Technology Co., Ltd. has multiple wholly owned onshore subsidiaries: Wuhan Lotus Cars Co., Ltd., Wuhan Lotus Cars Sales Limited, Wuhan Lotus Private Fund Management Co., Ltd., Sanya Lotus Venture Capital Co., Ltd., and Hangzhou Lotus Technology Service Co., Ltd.  
- **Row 5**: In addition to its onshore connections, Lotus Advanced Technology Limited also holds 100% ownership in several offshore subsidiaries: Lotus Tech Creative Centre Limited (UK), Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH (Germany), and Lotus Cars Europe B.V. (Netherlands).  
- **Row 6**: Lotus Technology Innovative Limited (UK) is another offshore subsidiary directly owned by Lotus Technology Inc.  

This corporate structure shows clear ownership chains and demarcates offshore and onshore entities within Lotus Technology Inc.’s operational framework.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Holding Company Structure and China Operations 
summary: The corporate structure of Lotus Technology Inc., as outlined in its annual report, reveals a comprehensive hierarchy that includes a parent holding company based in the Cayman Islands and various subsidiaries across offshore and onshore locations. The diagram illustrates the 100% equity ownership relationships among these entities, which include subsidiaries in Hong Kong, the UK, Germany, the Netherlands, and mainland China.

At the top of the structure is Lotus Technology Inc., which wholly owns two Hong Kong subsidiaries: Lotus Technology International Limited and Lotus Advanced Technology Limited. The latter manages Wuhan Lotus Technology Co., Ltd., an onshore subsidiary that oversees several others, including Wuhan Lotus Cars Co., Ltd. Additionally, Lotus Advanced Technology Limited owns several offshore subsidiaries, such as Lotus Tech Creative Centre Limited (UK), Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH (Germany), and Lotus Cars Europe B.V. (Netherlands). Another offshore entity, Lotus Technology Innovative Limited, is directly owned by Lotus Technology Inc.

The report highlights significant operational changes following a 2023 restructuring, which eliminated the previous Variable Interest Entity (VIE) structure. This restructuring allowed Lotus Technology Inc. to directly manage its operations in mainland China through its subsidiaries, enhancing its operational efficiency. The restructuring involved the WFOE acquiring full equity interests in former VIE subsidiaries and transferring associated assets and liabilities, thereby terminating the VIE Agreements.

Despite these structural improvements, the company faces considerable risks in conducting business in mainland China, including regulatory approvals for offshore offerings, antimonopoly actions, and cybersecurity concerns. These factors could adversely impact operations, limit foreign investments, and affect the company's ability to list on U.S. stock exchanges. The evolving legal landscape in China adds further uncertainty, with potential implications for business performance and the value of securities. Overall, while the restructuring aims to streamline operations, the company remains vigilant about the regulatory challenges inherent in the Chinese market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The existence of financial and personal interests of one or more of LCAA’s directors and officers results in conflicts of interest on the part of such director(s) between what those individuals may believe is in the best interests of LCAA and what those individuals may believe is best for themselves in determining to recommend that shareholders vote for the proposals.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Interests of LCAA’s Directors and Officers in the Business Combination 
summary: Shareholders of LCAA should carefully assess the potential conflicts of interest between the Sponsor and LCAA's directors and officers when considering the Business Combination and Merger Proposals. If LCAA does not complete the proposed Business Combination with LTC or another partner by the deadline, it will be forced to redeem all outstanding Public Shares for cash, leading to liquidation and dissolution. This scenario may incentivize the Sponsor to prioritize completing a deal—even with less favorable terms—over the interests of Public Shareholders to avoid liquidation.

In the event of a failed Business Combination, LCAA will cease operations, redeem Public Shares, and potentially dissolve, with the Sponsor's Founder Shares becoming worthless as they are not eligible for redemption. Conversely, a successful Business Combination would convert these Founder Shares into LTC Ordinary Shares. The Sponsor, who acquired the Founder Shares for $25,000, could see significant returns if the shares are valued at market prices, which could reach approximately $77.5 million based on recent trading prices.

The Sponsor also holds LCAA Private Warrants, which will be converted into LTC Warrants, and while the Sponsor stands to gain, Public Shareholders may face losses. The Sponsor and other Founder Shareholders have committed to vote in favor of the Business Combination and waive their redemption rights, indicating a strong alignment with the proposed merger despite potential adverse effects on Public Shareholders.

LCAA's Articles allow its officers and directors to pursue opportunities without presenting them to the company, which could create further conflicts of interest. The Sponsor has also agreed to forgo rights to liquidating distributions from the Trust Account if the Business Combination is not completed by the deadline. Additionally, the Sponsor may provide loans to LCAA for operational needs, which could be forgiven if the Business Combination does not occur.

To facilitate the Business Combination, the Sponsor has made financial commitments, including deposits to extend the deadline for the merger. They may also engage in transactions to acquire LCAA Public Shares from dissenting shareholders, potentially influencing the outcome of the vote on the Business Combination.

Overall, the interplay of financial interests, potential conflicts, and the differing priorities of the Sponsor and LCAA's leadership underscore the complexities shareholders must navigate when evaluating the proposed Business Combination and its implications for their investments.

There have also been concerns about the relationship between China and other countries, including the surrounding Asian countries, which may potentially have economic effects. In particular, there is significant uncertainty about the future relationship between the U.S. and China with respect to trade policies, treaties, government regulations, and tariffs. In addition, the COVID-19 pandemic has negatively impacted the economies of China, the U.S., and numerous other countries around the world. Economic conditions in jurisdictions where Lotus Technology operates may be sensitive to global economic conditions, as well as changes in domestic economic and political policies and the expected or perceived overall economic growth rate in specific jurisdictions. Any severe or prolonged economic slowdown globally and in any jurisdictions where Lotus Technology operates may materially and adversely affect the company's business, results of operations, and financial condition.

metadata.title_summary:
title: We and our manufacturing partner, Geely Holding, are subject to various environmental laws and regulations in jurisdictions we operate that could impose substantial costs upon us. 
summary: As an automobile developer, we, along with our manufacturing partner Geely Holding, are subject to a complex framework of environmental laws and regulations across the jurisdictions in which we operate. These regulations govern various aspects of our operations, including the use, handling, storage, and disposal of hazardous materials, fuel economy, emissions standards, and the construction and maintenance of manufacturing facilities. Key regulations impacting our operations include EU Regulation 715/2007, the Clean Air Act at the federal level in the U.S., various state-level regulations, and the Provisions on the Administration of Investments in the Automotive Industry in China.

Compliance with these environmental laws can lead to significant capital and operating expenses. Additionally, any violations could result in substantial fines, penalties, third-party damages, and operational disruptions, which could adversely affect our business prospects and operational results. The potential for future amendments or new environmental laws may necessitate further operational changes, potentially leading to material adverse effects on our business.

Moreover, unexpected delays in obtaining necessary environmental permits and approvals could hinder our operations, compounding the financial implications associated with compliance, remediation of contamination, and potential litigation arising from violations. Overall, the intricate landscape of environmental regulations presents both challenges and financial risks that could significantly impact our business operations and future growth.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: 1987 Esprit Type 85. Very few people seem to realize that this version of the Esprit was still being produced under the Type 85 designation, most assuming that all Esprit models were now coming from the factory with turbocharged engines and coded as the Type 82. In fact, the difference between the Esprit Turbo and the normally aspirated (n/a) base model was hard to spot for almost anybody except the real hard-core Lotus enthusiast. The new style car, code-named X180 by the factory, had been the work of a team of designers under the control of Lotus design director Colin Spooner and led by Peter Stevens, ably supported by Ken Sears. The fruits of their work were first seen when the new shape car was launched at the British Motor Show in October 1987, with delivery of the first cars going out to customers almost immediately. With the car being primarily a new bodyshell on top of an existing and well-proven chassis/engine combination, its development from start to finish had taken just 15 months, an incredibly short time to design what to the outside world seemed to be a new car.
